title: conformance route-tier check
purpose: G3.2 defines conformance route tier check for the agent-driven expert system. Verification & quality — evidence, goal_verify, anti-mistake.
scope: Owns `G3.2` only; siblings under `G3` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §9 — Branch G — Verification & quality plane (anti-mistake)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#9-branch-g-verification-quality-plane-anti-mistake).
behavior_steps: After H1 approves the plan, pursuit owns SDLC execution—implement, test, refactor, integrate, and deploy preparation—without requiring a human turn for each phase transition.

In-scope work runs through [A3.2](A3.2-goal-autopilot-until-goal-verify-or-hard-block.md): multi-role company workflows via template-packs, structured H2 blocker escalation, and platform-queue harness self-improvement alongside the active product goal.

Out-of-scope work—irreversible production without verify and rollback, silent waiver of safety gates, subjective aesthetic approval unless encoded in acceptance tests—must not enter autonomous pursuit; pack authors classify workflows against this boundary before H1.

When a step needs authority, judgment of record, or external access the system cannot obtain, preflight raises H2 per [INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md) instead of improvising past the three touchpoints.

If scope ambiguity would let agents advance past verify or waive gates silently, `check-pipeline-blocked.py` stops pursuit fail-closed until operators clarify boundaries via journal and state.json dual-write.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Flaky test â€” escalation S4 once, then H2 with evidence log; no silent retry loop.
Edge case `G3.2` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `G3.2`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Extend verify-router for goal-level suite invocation.
Wire CI: validate-workflow checks goal block when pursuit.mode=goal_autopilot.
Validate `G3.2` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `G3.2` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `G3.2`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: G3.2 defines conformance route tier check for the agent-driven expert system. Verification & quality — evidence, goal_verify, anti-mistake. In practice, the system after h1 approves the plan, pursuit owns sdlc execution—implement, test, refactor, integrate, and deploy preparation—without requiring a human turn for each phase transition, then in-scope work runs through [a3.2](a3.2-goal-autopilot-until-goal-verify-or-hard-block.md): multi-role company workflows via template-packs, structured h2 blocker escalation, and platform-queue harness self-improvement alongside the active product goal.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-g 2026-06-28 -->

route-tier conformance ensures `next_action`, `capability_class`, `spawn_workers`, and model tier align whenever the conductor changes pursuit direction. `python scripts/route-tier.py --apply` is the canonical apply path; validate-workflow cross-checks tier output against state.json.

Mis-routed genius implement or missing spawn_workers when task cards expect workers is a conformance failure, not a soft warning. Pack role bindings ([B5.2](B5.2-role-to-pipeline-id-skills-tool-permissions.md)) feed expected tier patterns for validation.
